title: Link
description: A link navigates users to another page, route, resource, or external destination.
whenToUse: Use links for navigation or opening resources. Use them when the result is a change of location rather than an immediate action.
whenNotToUse: Do not use links to submit forms or trigger in-place actions; use Button for actions.
acceptanceCriteria: Default, current-page, disabled, and open-in-new-window variants render from Figma matrix
Focus ring is visible when tabbed to
External links include target="_blank" and rel="noopener noreferrer"
Link is used only for navigation, not actions

props.name: target
props.description: Use _blank only when opening a new window or external resource.

props.name: aria-current
props.description: Marks the link as the current page.

props.name: aria-disabled
props.description: Marks link as disabled when navigation is unavailable.

contentGuidelines: Use concise destination-oriented text. Avoid vague labels like "click here".

dos: Use aria-current for the current page
Indicate new-window behavior visually and programmatically
donts: Do not use links for form submission
Do not disable links without explaining why
Do not open a new window unexpectedly

screenReader.announcement: Reads link text, current page when aria-current is set, and disabled when aria-disabled is true

wcag.howMet: Link purpose is clear from visible text
